findings.evidence: `CircleFundamentalGroup.lean:144-148` proves the generic private theorem; `CircleFundamentalGroup.lean:196-200` is the public real-only wrapper; `AddCircle.lean:172-175` already exposes the analogous generic `Deck.addCircleMulEquivInt`, and Mathlib’s `AddCircle.isCoveringMap_coe` is generic at `Mathlib/Topology/Covering/AddCircle.lean:25-33`.
findings.fix: Expose the generic non-torsion `ℤ` equivalence and its characterization lemmas, then make the real nonzero theorem a specialization.
findings.issue: `fundamentalGroupMulEquivOfAddCircle` gives the natural `FundamentalGroup (AddCircle p) x ≃* Multiplicative ℤ` statement for any topological additive group with `hcov` and `¬ IsOfFinAddOrder p`, but it is private; the public `fundamentalGroupMulEquiv` at line 196 specializes this to `p : ℝ` and `p ≠ 0`.

summary: The period-subgroup theorem is now public, but the infinite-cyclic specialization is still exposed only in the real case despite the file already proving the natural non-torsion version generically.
